stories.headline: Civil society groups oppose Escudero appointment as Sara Duterte impeachment trial presiding officer
stories.summary: Civil society groups including 1Sambayan and Youth Against Kurakot have opposed plans to designate Senator Francis Escudero as presiding officer in Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, set to begin July 6. The groups argued that the appointment could undermine the trial's constitutional credibility and that the role demands more than legal expertise.
stories.context: Escudero previously presided over an earlier Sara Duterte impeachment in 2025, which the Supreme Court dismissed. The current trial charges Duterte with misuse of P612.5 million in confidential funds, accumulation of unexplained wealth, and making threats against President Marcos and others.
stories.watch_for: The Sara Duterte impeachment trial is scheduled to begin July 6, 2026.

stories.headline: Rodrigo Duterte will not attend second ICC status conference on June 23
stories.summary: Former President Rodrigo Duterte has waived his right to attend the International Criminal Court's second status conference on June 23 in The Hague. The hearing will address procedural matters including expert panel reports on Duterte's health and scheduling of future proceedings. The ICC expects trial proceedings to begin on November 30.
stories.context: Duterte, arrested in March 2026 and transferred to The Hague, faces charges of crimes against humanity stemming from his anti-drug campaign. The ICC has jurisdiction over incidents between November 2011 and March 2019, when the Philippines was a Rome Statute signatory.
stories.watch_for: The ICC status conference is set for June 23. Trial proceedings are expected to begin November 30, 2026.

stories.headline: AFP and US forces complete West Philippine Sea drills while monitoring Chinese warships near Scarborough Shoal
stories.summary: The Armed Forces of the Philippines and the Philippine Coast Guard completed a six-day Maritime Cooperative Activity with US Indo-Pacific Command forces from June 14 to 19, the fourth bilateral exercise this year. During final patrols near Bajo de Masinloc, Philippine forces observed two PLA Navy frigates, a corvette, an auxiliary tanker, and a maritime patrol aircraft in the area. A previously documented Chinese floating platform inside the lagoon had been removed.

stories.headline: PAGASA expects Tropical Storm Mekkhala to enter PAR and be renamed Francisco, may strengthen to typhoon
stories.summary: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration reported that Tropical Storm Mekkhala, located about 1,590 kilometers east of Southeastern Luzon, was expected to enter the Philippine Area of Responsibility between Saturday evening and Sunday morning and receive the local name Francisco. The storm had maximum sustained winds of 85 kph with gusts up to 105 kph and was forecast to strengthen to typhoon intensity by Sunday.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA forecasts possible Wind Signal No. 1 over parts of Northern Luzon from June 23 to 24 as the storm approaches the country's northern coast.

stories.headline: Visayas electrical grid on yellow alert as multiple coal plants remain offline
stories.summary: The National Grid Corporation of the Philippines declared a yellow alert for the Visayas grid from 4 p.m. to 9 p.m. Saturday amid multiple coal plant outages, with 1,052 megawatts offline. Available capacity stood at 2,483 megawatts against projected peak demand of 2,382 megawatts, leaving insufficient operating reserve. NGCP said some plants have been unavailable since as far back as 2021.

stories.headline: Diesel prices expected to fall by up to P11 per liter next week as Middle East situation eases
stories.summary: Industry sources projected that diesel pump prices would decrease by P9 to P11 per liter next week and gasoline by P4 to P6 per liter, following stabilization in global oil markets linked to ongoing US-Iran diplomatic efforts. The Department of Energy offered more conservative estimates of P8 to P10 per liter for diesel. Current pump prices remain well above pre-February 2026 levels due to the Middle East conflict.

stories.headline: BSP lifts five-year digital transfer fee freeze, requires banks to justify charges transparently
stories.summary: The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas ended a five-year freeze on interbank digital transfer fees effective June 17, requiring banks to disclose and justify charges through transparent cost breakdowns rather than maintaining price controls. Philippine digital fees range from P9 to P30 per transaction, among the highest in Southeast Asia. New rules mandate that micro-transactions for merchants remain free and that receivers get the exact amount sent.

stories.headline: Philippine immigration agents arrest FBI most-wanted Medicare fraudster Herbert Kimble at Pasig casino
stories.summary: The Philippine Bureau of Immigration arrested Herbert Leon Kimble, named on the FBI's Most Wanted Fraudsters list, at a casino in Pasig City on June 11 and deported him on June 18. The US Department of Justice said Kimble orchestrated a $1.2 billion Medicare fraud scheme involving call centers in the Philippines and had fled before his scheduled sentencing in 2024.

stories.headline: Carlos Yulo wins floor exercise gold at Asian Gymnastics Championships, brother Eldrew takes bronze
stories.summary: Olympic double gold medalist Carlos Yulo claimed the floor exercise gold medal at the 2026 Asian Gymnastics Championships in Zunyi, China, with a score of 14.700 points. His younger brother Eldrew placed third with 14.300 points. Both brothers have also qualified for the horizontal bar finals at the same competition.

stories.headline: Western Mindanao naval forces and police seize nearly P99 million in smuggled cigarettes in Sulu
stories.summary: The Western Mindanao Naval Command intercepted two vessels carrying smuggled cigarettes worth about P94 million during maritime operations in Sulu on June 18. Philippine National Police in Jolo separately confiscated P5 million in contraband cigarettes from three abandoned vehicles on June 16. All seized cargo was turned over to the Bureau of Customs.

stories.headline: Taal Volcano emits elevated sulfur dioxide levels, PHIVOLCS maintains Alert Level 1
stories.summary: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology recorded 692 metric tons of sulfur dioxide from Taal Volcano's Main Crater over 24 hours, above baseline levels, with the plume reaching about 750 meters. Overall volcanic activity remained relatively stable with minimal seismic activity. Alert Level 1 remains in effect, and PHIVOLCS continues to advise against entry to the Taal Volcano Island.

stories.headline: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz as US-Iran nuclear talks prepare to open in Switzerland
stories.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and warned shipping to stay away, accusing the United States and Israel of violating a ceasefire memorandum of understanding signed earlier in the week. US-Iran nuclear talks were nonetheless scheduled to begin Sunday in Switzerland, with Pakistani and Qatari mediators alongside US negotiators Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff.
stories.context: A ceasefire memorandum signed by US President Trump and Iranian President Pezeshkian called for an end to military operations and required a final deal within 60 days. Fighting continued in Lebanon despite a ceasefire that took effect Friday, with Lebanese officials reporting dozens of casualties from Israeli strikes.
stories.watch_for: US-Iran nuclear talks are scheduled to begin Sunday, June 21, at Burgenstock resort in Switzerland.

stories.headline: Russian strikes kill five in Zaporizhzhia as Ukraine hits oil refinery inside Russia
stories.summary: Russian glide bomb attacks on Zaporizhzhia killed five civilians and injured 11, according to regional Governor Ivan Fedorov. Separate strikes in Kharkiv killed one and wounded nine while drone attacks hit Kherson. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned that Russia was preparing a massive attack and said Ukrainian forces had struck an oil refinery in Russia's Tyumen region.

stories.headline: Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah killed in Israeli air strike on Bureij refugee camp in Gaza
stories.summary: Ahmed Wishah, a cameraman for Al Jazeera Mubasher, was killed in an Israeli air strike on a house in Gaza's Bureij refugee camp. The Israeli military alleged without providing evidence that Wishah had been a combatant. Al Jazeera said at least 260 Palestinian journalists have been killed since October 2023, including 12 working for the network. Gaza's health ministry reported 73,018 total deaths since the conflict began.
stories.context: Wishah's brother Mohammed, also an Al Jazeera journalist, was killed in an Israeli strike in April 2026. The Committee to Protect Journalists has documented a pattern of such allegations against slain journalists without credible evidence.

stories.headline: Bolivia declares 90-day state of emergency as president authorizes military to clear roadblocks
stories.summary: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz declared a 90-day state of emergency granting the military authority to dismantle roadblocks that have disrupted food and fuel supplies for 50 days. The unrest, led by Indigenous highland and rural workers opposing Paz's elimination of fuel subsidies, has resulted in at least 17 deaths, 37 injuries, and 365 arrests. A labor union agreement was reached Friday but some demonstrators continued demanding Paz's resignation.
stories.context: Paz took office in November 2025 and pledged economic recovery, but his austerity policies including the removal of fuel subsidies have worsened inflation in the country.
